# Gradient Programming Language - Complete Specification

> Version: v0.1
> Target audience: AI agents requiring complete language specification

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Complete EBNF Grammar](#1-complete-ebnf-grammar)
2. [Type System - Full Specification](#2-type-system-full-specification)
3. [Effect System - Full Specification](#3-effect-system-full-specification)
4. [Memory Model (3 Tiers)](#4-memory-model-3-tiers)
5. [Actor Model Specification](#5-actor-model-specification)
6. [Standard Library - Complete Signatures](#6-standard-library-complete-signatures)
7. [Example Programs](#7-example-programs)

---

## 1. Complete EBNF Grammar

```ebnf
# ============================================================================
# Gradient Programming Language -- Complete Formal EBNF Grammar
# ============================================================================

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Program structure
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

program        ::= [ module_decl ] { NEWLINE } { use_decl NEWLINE { NEWLINE } } { top_item { NEWLINE } } EOF

module_decl    ::= "mod" module_path NEWLINE

module_path    ::= IDENT { "." IDENT }

use_decl       ::= "use" module_path [ "." "{" use_list "}" ] NEWLINE

use_list       ::= IDENT { "," IDENT } [ "," ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Top-level items
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

top_item       ::= cap_decl
                  | { annotation } ( fn_def | let_decl | type_decl | extern_fn_decl )

annotation     ::= "@" IDENT [ "(" annotation_args ")" ] NEWLINE

annotation_args ::= expr { "," expr } [ "," ]

cap_decl       ::= "@" "cap" "(" cap_args ")" NEWLINE

cap_args       ::= IDENT { "," IDENT } [ "," ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Function definitions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

fn_def         ::= "fn" IDENT "(" [ param_list ] ")" [ return_clause ] ":" NEWLINE block

return_clause  ::= "->" [ effect_set ] type_expr

param_list     ::= param { "," param } [ "," ]

param          ::= IDENT ":" type_expr

extern_fn_decl ::= "@extern" NEWLINE "fn" IDENT "(" [ param_list ] ")" [ return_clause ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Statements and blocks
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

block          ::= INDENT stmt { stmt } DEDENT

stmt           ::= ( let_stmt | assign_stmt | ret_stmt | expr_stmt ) NEWLINE

let_stmt       ::= "let" [ "mut" ] IDENT [ ":" type_expr ] "=" expr

let_decl       ::= let_stmt

assign_stmt    ::= IDENT "=" expr

ret_stmt       ::= "ret" expr

expr_stmt      ::= expr


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Control flow (all are expressions)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

if_expr        ::= "if" expr ":" NEWLINE block
                    { "else" "if" expr ":" NEWLINE block }
                    [ "else" ":" NEWLINE block ]

for_expr       ::= "for" IDENT "in" expr ":" NEWLINE block

while_expr     ::= "while" expr ":" NEWLINE block

match_expr     ::= "match" expr ":" NEWLINE INDENT match_arm { match_arm } DEDENT

match_arm      ::= pattern ":" NEWLINE block

pattern        ::= [ "-" ] INT_LIT
                  | BOOL_LIT
                  | "_"
                  | IDENT [ "(" IDENT ")" ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Type declarations (aliases and enums)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

type_decl      ::= "type" IDENT "=" ( enum_variants | type_expr ) NEWLINE

enum_variants  ::= variant { "|" variant }

variant        ::= IDENT [ "(" type_expr ")" ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Expressions -- precedence climbing (lowest to highest)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

expr           ::= if_expr
                  | for_expr
                  | while_expr
                  | match_expr
                  | or_expr

or_expr        ::= and_expr { "or" and_expr }

and_expr       ::= not_expr { "and" not_expr }

not_expr       ::= "not" not_expr
                  | cmp_expr

cmp_expr       ::= add_expr [ cmp_op add_expr ]

cmp_op         ::= "==" | "!=" | "<=" | ">=" | "<" | ">"

add_expr       ::= mul_expr { ( "+" | "-" ) mul_expr }

mul_expr       ::= unary_expr { ( "*" | "/" | "%" ) unary_expr }

unary_expr     ::= "-" unary_expr
                  | postfix_expr

postfix_expr   ::= atom { call_args | "." IDENT }

call_args      ::= "(" [ arg_list ] ")"

arg_list       ::= expr { "," expr } [ "," ]

atom           ::= FLOAT_LIT
                  | INT_LIT
                  | STRING_LIT
                  | BOOL_LIT
                  | UNIT_LIT
                  | typed_hole
                  | IDENT
                  | "(" expr ")"

typed_hole     ::= "?" [ IDENT ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Type expressions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

type_expr      ::= type_atom

type_atom      ::= UNIT_TYPE | IDENT | effect_function_type

UNIT_TYPE      ::= "(" ")"

effect_function_type ::= "fn" "(" [ type_expr { "," type_expr } ] ")" [ effect_set ] type_expr

effect_set     ::= "!" "{" IDENT { "," IDENT } [ "," ] "}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Lexical terminals
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

IDENT          ::= IDENT_START { IDENT_CONT }
IDENT_START    ::= LETTER | "_"
IDENT_CONT     ::= LETTER | DIGIT | "_"
LETTER         ::= "a" | "b" | ... | "z" | "A" | "B" | ... | "Z"
DIGIT          ::= "0" | "1" | ... | "9"

INT_LIT        ::= DIGIT { DIGIT | "_" }

FLOAT_LIT      ::= DIGIT { DIGIT | "_" } "." DIGIT { DIGIT | "_" }

STRING_LIT     ::= '"' { STRING_CHAR } '"'
STRING_CHAR    ::= ESCAPE_SEQ | REGULAR_CHAR
ESCAPE_SEQ     ::= "\\" ( "n" | "r" | "t" | "\\" | '"' | "0" )
REGULAR_CHAR   ::= <any ASCII except '"' and '\\'>

BOOL_LIT       ::= "true" | "false"

UNIT_LIT       ::= "(" ")"

COMMENT        ::= "//" { <any except newline> }

# Keywords (reserved, cannot be used as IDENT):
# fn, let, mut, if, else, for, while, in, ret, type, mod, use,
# match, true, false, and, or, not, impl
# Reserved for actors: actor, spawn, send, ask, state, on

# Synthetic tokens (emitted by lexer):
# INDENT, DEDENT, NEWLINE, EOF
```

---

## 2. Type System - Full Specification

### 2.1 Type Hierarchy

```
Type ::=
    | PrimitiveType
    | UserDefinedType
    | FunctionType
    | EffectFunctionType
    | UnitType

PrimitiveType ::=
    | Int          (64-bit signed)
    | Float        (64-bit IEEE 754)
    | String       (UTF-8 immutable)
    | Bool         (true | false)

UserDefinedType ::=
    | EnumType
    | TypeAlias

EnumType ::= IDENT "=" Variant { "|" Variant }
Variant ::= IDENT [ "(" Type ")" ]

TypeAlias ::= IDENT "=" Type

FunctionType ::= "fn" "(" [ Type { "," Type } ] ")" "->" Type

EffectFunctionType ::= "fn" "(" [ Type { "," Type } ] ")" "->" EffectSet Type

EffectSet ::= "!" "{" IDENT { "," IDENT } "}"

UnitType ::= "()"
```

### 2.2 Hindley-Milner Type Inference Rules

**Rule T-Var (Variable):**
```
Γ(x) = τ
-----------
Γ ⊢ x : τ
```

**Rule T-Const (Constant):**
```
-----------------
Γ ⊢ n : Int      (n is integer literal)
Γ ⊢ s : String   (s is string literal)
Γ ⊢ b : Bool     (b is boolean literal)
```

**Rule T-Let (Let Binding):**
```
Γ ⊢ e₁ : τ₁    Γ, x:τ₁ ⊢ e₂ : τ₂
--------------------------------
Γ ⊢ let x = e₁ in e₂ : τ₂

With explicit annotation:
Γ ⊢ e₁ : τ    Γ, x:τ ⊢ e₂ : τ₂
-------------------------------
Γ ⊢ let x:τ = e₁ in e₂ : τ₂
```

**Rule T-Fn (Function Definition):**
```
Γ, x₁:τ₁, ..., xₙ:τₙ ⊢ body : τ_ret
------------------------------------------------
Γ ⊢ fn f(x₁:τ₁, ..., xₙ:τₙ) -> τ_ret: body : fn(τ₁,...,τₙ) -> τ_ret
```

**Rule T-App (Function Application):**
```
Γ ⊢ f : fn(τ₁,...,τₙ) -> τ_ret    Γ ⊢ e₁ : τ₁    ...    Γ ⊢ eₙ : τₙ
-------------------------------------------------------------------
Γ ⊢ f(e₁,...,eₙ) : τ_ret
```

**Rule T-If (Conditional):**
```
Γ ⊢ cond : Bool    Γ ⊢ then : τ    Γ ⊢ else : τ
------------------------------------------------
Γ ⊢ if cond: then else: else : τ
```

**Rule T-Match (Pattern Match):**
```
Γ ⊢ scrutinee : τ_scrut    ∀i. Γ, pattern_i : τ_scrut ⊢ arm_i : τ
-----------------------------------------------------------------
Γ ⊢ match scrutinee: arm₁ ... armₙ : τ
```

### 2.3 Type Inference Examples

**Example 1: Factorial with inferred types**
```gradient
fn factorial(n: Int) -> Int:
    if n <= 1:
        ret 1
    else:
        ret n * factorial(n - 1)
```
Inference steps:
- `n` has type `Int` (annotated)
- `1` has type `Int`
- `n <= 1` has type `Bool` (comparison)
- `ret 1` returns `Int`
- `n * factorial(...)` - `n` is `Int`, recursive call returns `Int`, result is `Int`
- Both branches return `Int`, so `if` expression is `Int`
- Function returns `Int` ✓

**Example 2: Let binding with type inference**
```gradient
let x = 42          // x : Int (inferred)
let y = x + 1       // y : Int (from Int + Int)
let msg = "hi"      // msg : String (inferred)
```

**Example 3: Polymorphic functions (future)**
```gradient
fn identity(x: a) -> a:
    ret x
// a is a type variable, identity works for any type
```

### 2.4 Unification Algorithm

The type checker uses unification to solve type constraints:

```
unify(τ₁, τ₂):
    if τ₁ == τ₂: return
    if τ₁ is variable: bind τ₁ = τ₂
    if τ₂ is variable: bind τ₂ = τ₁
    if both are function types:
        unify parameter types (covariant)
        unify return type (contravariant)
    else: type error
```

**Occurs Check:** Prevents infinite types (e.g., `a = List[a]`)

### 2.5 Type Safety Properties

1. **Progress:** Well-typed programs don't get stuck (every expression either is a value or can reduce)
2. **Preservation:** If `e : τ` and `e` reduces to `e'`, then `e' : τ`
3. **Exhaustive match checking:** `match` expressions must cover all variants
4. **No null:** `Option[T]` used explicitly for nullable values

---

## 3. Effect System - Full Specification

### 3.1 Effect Types

Effects are tracked in the type system using row polymorphism:

```
EffectRow ::= 
    | { ε₁, ε₂, ..., εₙ }      -- Closed row (exact effects)
    | { ε₁, ..., εₙ | ρ }      -- Open row (plus unknown effects ρ)
```

**Canonical effects (v0.1):**
| Effect | Description | Operations |
|--------|-------------|------------|
| `IO` | Input/Output | `print`, `read_line` |
| `Net` | Network | `http_get`, `socket_read` |
| `FS` | Filesystem | `file_read`, `file_write` |
| `Mut` | Mutation | Assignment to shared state |
| `Time` | Time/Date | `now`, `sleep` |

### 3.2 Effect Inference Rules

**Rule E-Pure (Pure Expression):**
```
Γ ⊢ e : τ    e contains no effectful operations
-----------------------------------------------
Γ ⊢ e : pure τ
```

**Rule E-Effectful (Effect Propagation):**
```
Γ ⊢ e₁ : !{ε₁} τ₁    Γ, x:τ₁ ⊢ e₂ : !{ε₂} τ₂
------------------------------------------------
Γ ⊢ let x = e₁ in e₂ : !{ε₁ ∪ ε₂} τ₂
```

**Rule E-Fun (Effect Polymorphism):**
```
Γ, f:fn(τ₁)->!{ε}τ₂, x:τ₁ ⊢ body : !{ε'} τ
------------------------------------------------
Γ ⊢ fn f(x:τ₁) -> !{ε ∪ ε'} τ: body : fn(τ₁)->!{ε ∪ ε'}τ
```

### 3.3 Effect Checking Algorithm

```python
def infer_effects(expr, env):
    match expr:
        case Var(x):
            return set()  # Pure
        case Call(f, args):
            f_effects = infer_effects(f, env)
            arg_effects = union(infer_effects(a, env) for a in args)
            return f_effects.union(arg_effects)
        case Let(x, e1, e2):
            return infer_effects(e1, env).union(infer_effects(e2, env))
        case If(cond, then, else_):
            return infer_effects(cond, env).union(
                infer_effects(then, env),
                infer_effects(else_, env)
            )
        case EffectfulOp(op):
            return {op.effect}
```

### 3.4 Effect Examples

**Example 1: Pure function**
```gradient
fn square(n: Int) -> Int:
    ret n * n
// Effects: {} (empty set, compiler-proven pure)
// Symbol table: is_pure: true
```

**Example 2: Effectful function**
```gradient
fn greet(name: String) -> !{IO} ():
    print("Hello, " + name)
// Effects: {IO}
```

**Example 3: Effect propagation**
```gradient
fn log_and_compute(x: Int) -> !{IO} Int:
    print_int(x)          // {IO}
    ret x * 2             // {}
// Combined effects: {IO} ∪ {} = {IO}
```

**Example 4: Higher-order function (polymorphic)**
```gradient
fn apply_twice(f: fn(Int) -> Int, x: Int) -> Int:
    ret f(f(x))
// Effects: same as f's effects (row polymorphic)
// If f is pure, apply_twice is pure
// If f has {IO}, apply_twice has {IO}
```

### 3.5 Module Capability Constraints

```gradient
@cap(IO, Net)
mod server

fn handler() -> !{IO} ():      // OK: within cap
    print("request")

fn fetch() -> !{Net} String:   // OK: within cap
    http_get("api.example.com")

fn save() -> !{FS} ():         // ERROR: FS not in @cap
    file_write("log.txt", "data")
```

---

## 4. Memory Model (3 Tiers)

### 4.1 Tier 1: Arena Allocation

**Properties:**
- Default allocation strategy
- Bulk deallocation at scope exit
- Zero annotation burden
- Compile-time ownership inference (Lobster-style)

**Syntax:**
```gradient
fn process() -> !{IO} ():
    let arena = Arena.new()
    let data = arena.alloc(MyStruct)  // Allocated in arena
    // ... use data ...
    defer arena.deinit()              // All arena memory freed
```

**Semantics:**
- Memory is allocated from the arena's contiguous buffer
- No individual deallocation (can't free single objects)
- Entire arena freed at once when scope exits
- Thread-local by default

### 4.2 Tier 2: Generational References

**Properties:**
- Runtime generation checking
- 8 bytes overhead per allocation (generation number)
- Checked at dereference
- No compile-time annotations required

**Syntax:**
```gradient
fn build_graph() -> Graph:
    let g = Graph.new()
    let n1 = g.add_node(1)
    let n2 = g.add_node(2)
    g.add_edge(n1, n2)    // Safe even with aliasing
    ret g
// Each reference carries generation; freed objects marked
// Dereferencing freed generation = runtime error (not UB)
```

**Safety mechanism:**
```
Allocation:   [ptr | gen=42]
              ↓
Dereference:  check gen == current_gen
              if mismatch: trap (defined error)
Free:         mark generation as invalid
```

### 4.3 Tier 3: Linear Types

**Properties:**
- Austral-style linear types
- Must be used exactly once
- Compile-time enforcement
- For kernel/driver code

**Syntax:**
```gradient
@linear
fn acquire_resource() -> Resource:
    // Returns linear resource

fn use_resource(r: Resource) -> ():  // Consumes r
    // ... use r ...

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    let r = acquire_resource()  // r : Resource (linear)
    use_resource(r)               // r consumed here
    // use_resource(r)            // ERROR: r already consumed
```

**Linear type rules:**
1. Linear values must be used exactly once
2. Cannot discard (must explicitly destroy or transfer)
3. Cannot duplicate (no aliasing)
4. Branching: must use in exactly one branch

### 4.4 Memory Safety Comparison

| Model | Safety | Annotations | Runtime Cost | Use Case |
|-------|--------|-------------|--------------|----------|
| Arena | Safe deallocation | None | None | 80% of code |
| Generational | Use-after-free prevention | None | 8 bytes + check | Graphs, 15% |
| Linear | Full control | Moderate | None | Kernels, 5% |

---

## 5. Actor Model Specification

### 5.1 Actor Definition

```gradient
actor ChatServer:
    state users: Map[UserId, User]
    state rooms: Map[RoomId, Room]
    
    on Join(user: User, room: RoomId):
        // Handle join message
        
    on Leave(user: User):
        // Handle leave message
        
    on Message(user: User, content: String):
        // Broadcast message
```

### 5.2 Actor Lifecycle

```
1. SPAWN:  let actor_ref = spawn ActorType(initial_state)
2. SEND:   actor_ref ! Message(args)     // Fire-and-forget
3. ASK:    let result = ask actor_ref Query(args)  // Request-response
4. TERMINATE:  stop actor_ref            // Graceful shutdown
```

### 5.3 Reference Capabilities (Pony-style)

Actors use reference capabilities for safe message passing:

| Capability | Aliasing | Mutability | Sendable | Use |
|------------|----------|------------|----------|-----|
| `iso` | No aliases | Mutable | Yes | Transfer between actors |
| `val` | Many aliases | Immutable | Yes | Share read-only data |
| `ref` | Many aliases | Mutable | No | Actor-local mutable state |
| `box` | Many aliases | Read-only | No | Read-only view |
| `trn` | No aliases | Mutable | No | Unique mutable reference |
| `tag` | Opaque | Opaque | Yes | Actor identity only |

### 5.4 Supervision Trees

```gradient
@supervision(strategy: one_for_one, max_restarts: 3, window: 60s)
actor Supervisor:
    let workers = [
        spawn Worker(name: "worker1"),
        spawn Worker(name: "worker2"),
        spawn Worker(name: "worker3")
    ]
    
    on ChildFailed(child: ActorRef, error: Error):
        match error:
            Recoverable:
                restart(child)
            Fatal:
                escalate(error)
```

**Supervision strategies:**
- `one_for_one` - Restart only failed child
- `one_for_all` - Restart all children
- `rest_for_one` - Restart failed and younger siblings

### 5.5 Structured Concurrency

```gradient
fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    with_scope:
        let task1 = spawn async_task()
        let task2 = spawn async_task()
        // Both tasks complete or are cancelled when scope exits
    // Guaranteed: both tasks finished
```

**Properties:**
- Child tasks cannot outlive parent scope
- Automatic cancellation propagation
- Error propagation to parent
- Backpressure via mailbox limits

### 5.6 Effect System for Actors

```gradient
actor FileProcessor:
    @requires(file.exists())
    fn process(path: Path) -> !{FS, IO} Result:
        let content = file_read(path)
        analyze(content)
        
// Actor's capabilities determine allowed effects
// spawn FileProcessor with FS capability grants FS permission
```

---

## 6. Standard Library - Complete Signatures

### 6.1 Core Builtins (Global Scope)

```gradient
// Printing
fn print(s: String) -> !{IO} ()
fn println(s: String) -> !{IO} ()
fn print_int(n: Int) -> !{IO} ()
fn print_float(f: Float) -> !{IO} ()
fn print_bool(b: Bool) -> !{IO} ()

// Math
fn abs(n: Int) -> Int
fn min(a: Int, b: Int) -> Int
fn max(a: Int, b: Int) -> Int
fn min_float(a: Float, b: Float) -> Float
fn max_float(a: Float, b: Float) -> Float
fn sqrt(n: Float) -> Float
fn pow(base: Float, exp: Float) -> Float
fn mod_int(a: Int, b: Int) -> Int
fn floor(f: Float) -> Int
fn ceil(f: Float) -> Int

// String
fn to_string(n: Int) -> String
fn int_to_string(n: Int) -> String
fn float_to_string(f: Float) -> String
fn string_length(s: String) -> Int
fn string_concat(a: String, b: String) -> String
fn string_slice(s: String, start: Int, end: Int) -> String
fn string_contains(haystack: String, needle: String) -> Bool
fn string_starts_with(s: String, prefix: String) -> Bool
fn string_ends_with(s: String, suffix: String) -> Bool

// Conversion
fn int_to_float(n: Int) -> Float
fn float_to_int(f: Float) -> Int

// Comparison
fn compare_int(a: Int, b: Int) -> Int     // -1, 0, 1
fn compare_float(a: Float, b: Float) -> Int

// Iteration
fn range(end: Int) -> Range
fn range_from(start: Int, end: Int) -> Range

// Panic/Error
fn panic(msg: String) -> !     // Divergent, never returns
fn unreachable() -> !          // Marks unreachable code
```

### 6.2 std.io Module

```gradient
// Console I/O
fn read_line() -> !{IO} String
fn read_char() -> !{IO} String
fn prompt(msg: String) -> !{IO} String

// File I/O
fn file_read(path: String) -> !{FS, IO} String
fn file_write(path: String, content: String) -> !{FS, IO} ()
fn file_exists(path: String) -> !{FS} Bool
fn file_delete(path: String) -> !{FS, IO} ()

// Error handling variants
fn file_read_or_default(path: String, default: String) -> !{FS, IO} String
```

### 6.3 std.collections Module

```gradient
// List (dynamic array)
type List[T]
fn list_new[T]() -> List[T]
fn list_push[T](lst: List[T], item: T) -> List[T]
fn list_pop[T](lst: List[T]) -> Option[T]
fn list_get[T](lst: List[T], idx: Int) -> Option[T]
fn list_set[T](lst: List[T], idx: Int, item: T) -> List[T]
fn list_length[T](lst: List[T]) -> Int
fn list_map[T, U](lst: List[T], f: fn(T) -> U) -> List[U]
fn list_filter[T](lst: List[T], pred: fn(T) -> Bool) -> List[T]
fn list_fold[T, U](lst: List[T], init: U, f: fn(U, T) -> U) -> U

// Map (hash map)
type Map[K, V]
fn map_new[K, V]() -> Map[K, V]
fn map_insert[K, V](m: Map[K, V], key: K, value: V) -> Map[K, V]
fn map_get[K, V](m: Map[K, V], key: K) -> Option[V]
fn map_remove[K, V](m: Map[K, V], key: K) -> Map[K, V]
fn map_contains[K, V](m: Map[K, V], key: K) -> Bool
fn map_keys[K, V](m: Map[K, V]) -> List[K]
fn map_values[K, V](m: Map[K, V]) -> List[V]

// Set
type Set[T]
fn set_new[T]() -> Set[T]
fn set_insert[T](s: Set[T], item: T) -> Set[T]
fn set_remove[T](s: Set[T], item: T) -> Set[T]
fn set_contains[T](s: Set[T], item: T) -> Bool
fn set_union[T](a: Set[T], b: Set[T]) -> Set[T]
fn set_intersection[T](a: Set[T], b: Set[T]) -> Set[T]
fn set_difference[T](a: Set[T], b: Set[T]) -> Set[T]
```

### 6.4 std.option Module

```gradient
type Option[T] = Some(T) | None

fn is_some[T](opt: Option[T]) -> Bool
fn is_none[T](opt: Option[T]) -> Bool
fn unwrap[T](opt: Option[T]) -> T                   // Panics if None
fn unwrap_or[T](opt: Option[T], default: T) -> T
fn unwrap_or_else[T](opt: Option[T], f: fn() -> T) -> T
fn map_option[T, U](opt: Option[T], f: fn(T) -> U) -> Option[U]
fn flat_map[T, U](opt: Option[T], f: fn(T) -> Option[U]) -> Option[U]
fn filter[T](opt: Option[T], pred: fn(T) -> Bool) -> Option[T]
```

### 6.5 std.result Module

```gradient
type Result[T, E] = Ok(T) | Err(E)

fn is_ok[T, E](res: Result[T, E]) -> Bool
fn is_err[T, E](res: Result[T, E]) -> Bool
fn unwrap[T, E](res: Result[T, E]) -> T           // Panics if Err
fn unwrap_or[T, E](res: Result[T, E], default: T) -> T
fn unwrap_err[T, E](res: Result[T, E]) -> E
fn map_result[T, E, U](res: Result[T, E], f: fn(T) -> U) -> Result[U, E]
fn map_err[T, E, F](res: Result[T, E], f: fn(E) -> F) -> Result[T, F]
fn and_then[T, E, U](res: Result[T, E], f: fn(T) -> Result[U, E]) -> Result[U, E]
fn or_else[T, E, U](res: Result[T, E], f: fn(E) -> Result[T, U]) -> Result[T, U]
```

### 6.6 std.string Module

```gradient
fn trim(s: String) -> String
fn trim_left(s: String) -> String
fn trim_right(s: String) -> String
fn to_uppercase(s: String) -> String
fn to_lowercase(s: String) -> String
fn split(s: String, delimiter: String) -> List[String]
fn join(parts: List[String], separator: String) -> String
fn replace(s: String, from: String, to: String) -> String
fn parse_int(s: String) -> Result[Int, String]
fn parse_float(s: String) -> Result[Float, String]
```

### 6.7 std.time Module

```gradient
fn now() -> !{Time} Timestamp
fn sleep_ms(millis: Int) -> !{Time, IO} ()
fn timestamp_to_string(ts: Timestamp) -> String
fn elapsed_ms(start: Timestamp, end: Timestamp) -> Int
```

### 6.8 std.net Module

```gradient
fn http_get(url: String) -> !{Net, IO} Result[String, String]
fn http_post(url: String, body: String) -> !{Net, IO} Result[String, String]
fn tcp_connect(host: String, port: Int) -> !{Net, IO} Result[Socket, String]
fn tcp_listen(port: Int) -> !{Net, IO} Result[Listener, String]
```

### 6.9 std.actor Module

```gradient
fn spawn[A](initial: A) -> ActorRef[A]
fn send[A](ref: ActorRef[A], msg: Message) -> !{IO} ()
fn ask[A, R](ref: ActorRef[A], msg: Message) -> !{IO} Result[R, Timeout]
fn stop[A](ref: ActorRef[A]) -> !{IO} ()
fn link(parent: ActorRef, child: ActorRef) -> !{IO} ()
```

---

## 7. Example Programs

### 7.1 Hello World

```gradient
// hello.gr -- The minimal Gradient program
mod hello

use std.io

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    print("Hello, world!")
```

### 7.2 Factorial (Recursive and Tail-Recursive)

```gradient
// factorial.gr -- Demonstrates recursion and mutability
mod factorial

use std.io

// Recursive version
fn factorial(n: Int) -> Int:
    if n <= 1:
        ret 1
    else:
        ret n * factorial(n - 1)

// Tail-recursive helper
fn factorial_tail(n: Int, acc: Int) -> Int:
    if n <= 1:
        ret acc
    else:
        ret factorial_tail(n - 1, n * acc)

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    let n = 10
    let result = factorial(n)
    let result2 = factorial_tail(n, 1)
    
    print_int(result)
    print_int(result2)
    
    if result == result2:
        print("Both methods agree!")
    else:
        print("Mismatch!")
```

### 7.3 Pattern Matching with Enums

```gradient
// patterns.gr -- Algebraic data types and pattern matching
mod patterns

use std.io

type Color = Red | Green | Blue
type Shape = Circle(Float) | Rectangle(Float, Float) | Square(Float)
type OptionInt = SomeInt(Int) | NoneInt

fn color_name(c: Color) -> String:
    match c:
        Red:
            "red"
        Green:
            "green"
        Blue:
            "blue"

fn shape_area(s: Shape) -> Float:
    match s:
        Circle(r):
            3.14159 * r * r
        Rectangle(w, h):
            w * h
        Square(s):
            s * s

fn safe_divide(a: Int, b: Int) -> OptionInt:
    if b == 0:
        ret NoneInt
    else:
        ret SomeInt(a / b)

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    let c = Red
    print(color_name(c))
    
    let area = shape_area(Rectangle(3.0, 4.0))
    print_float(area)
    
    match safe_divide(10, 2):
        SomeInt(n):
            print_int(n)
        NoneInt:
            print("Division by zero!")
```

### 7.4 Mutable State with While Loop

```gradient
// counter.gr -- Mutable bindings and iteration
mod counter

use std.io

fn countdown(from: Int) -> !{IO} ():
    let mut n = from
    while n > 0:
        print_int(n)
        n = n - 1
    print("Liftoff!")

fn sum_range(n: Int) -> Int:
    let mut sum = 0
    let mut i = 1
    while i <= n:
        sum = sum + i
        i = i + 1
    ret sum

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    countdown(10)
    let total = sum_range(100)
    print_int(total)
```

### 7.5 Higher-Order Functions

```gradient
// higher_order.gr -- Functions as values
mod higher_order

use std.io

fn apply(f: fn(Int) -> Int, x: Int) -> Int:
    ret f(x)

fn twice(f: fn(Int) -> Int, x: Int) -> Int:
    ret f(f(x))

fn make_adder(n: Int) -> fn(Int) -> Int:
    // Returns a closure that adds n
    ret fn(x: Int) -> Int:
        ret x + n

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    let double = fn(x: Int) -> Int:
        ret x * 2
    
    let result1 = apply(double, 5)      // 10
    let result2 = twice(double, 3)      // 12
    let add5 = make_adder(5)
    let result3 = add5(10)              // 15
    
    print_int(result1)
    print_int(result2)
    print_int(result3)
```

### 7.6 Contract-Driven Design

```gradient
// contracts.gr -- Pre/Post conditions
mod contracts

use std.io

@requires(n >= 0)
@ensures(result >= 1)
fn factorial(n: Int) -> Int:
    if n <= 1:
        ret 1
    else:
        ret n * factorial(n - 1)

@requires(b != 0)
@ensures(result * b == a)  // Exact division
fn divide(a: Int, b: Int) -> Int:
    ret a / b

@requires(list_length(lst) > 0)
@ensures(is_some(result))
fn first[T](lst: List[T]) -> Option[T]:
    ret list_get(lst, 0)

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    let f5 = factorial(5)       // 120
    print_int(f5)
    
    let q = divide(10, 2)       // 5
    print_int(q)
    // divide(10, 0) would fail contract at runtime
```

### 7.7 Simple Actor

```gradient
// actor_example.gr -- Actor-based concurrency
mod actor_example

use std.io
use std.actor

actor Counter:
    state count: Int = 0
    
    on Increment:
        count = count + 1
        
    on GetCount:
        ret count
        
    on Reset:
        count = 0

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    let counter = spawn(Counter)
    
    send(counter, Increment)
    send(counter, Increment)
    send(counter, Increment)
    
    let current = ask(counter, GetCount)
    print_int(current)          // 3
    
    send(counter, Reset)
```

### 7.8 File Processing Pipeline

```gradient
// file_pipeline.gr -- Effectful file operations
mod file_pipeline

use std.io
use std.string

@requires(file_exists(input_path))
fn process_file(input_path: String) -> !{FS, IO} String:
    let content = file_read(input_path)
    let lines = split(content, "\n")
    let trimmed = map(lines, trim)
    let non_empty = filter(trimmed, fn(s) { s != "" })
    ret join(non_empty, "\n")

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    if file_exists("input.txt"):
        let result = process_file("input.txt")
        file_write("output.txt", result)
        print("Processing complete!")
    else:
        print("Input file not found!")
```

### 7.9 Error Handling with Result

```gradient
// error_handling.gr -- Robust error handling
mod error_handling

use std.io
use std.result
use std.string

fn parse_config(content: String) -> Result[Config, String]:
    let parts = split(content, "=")
    if list_length(parts) != 2:
        ret Err("Invalid config format")
    
    let key = unwrap(list_get(parts, 0))
    let value = unwrap(list_get(parts, 1))
    
    if key == "" or value == "":
        ret Err("Empty key or value")
    
    ret Ok(Config(key, value))

fn load_and_parse(path: String) -> !{FS, IO} Result[Config, String]:
    if not file_exists(path):
        ret Err("File not found")
    
    let content = file_read(path)
    ret parse_config(content)

fn main() -> !{IO} ():
    match load_and_parse("config.txt"):
        Ok(cfg):
            print("Loaded config: " + cfg.key)
        Err(e):
            print("Error: " + e)
```

---

## Appendix: Compiler API Schema

### JSON Output Schemas

**Diagnostic Schema:**
```json
{
  "errors": [
    {
      "phase": "parse|type|ir",
      "severity": "error|warning",
      "message": "string",
      "span": {"file": "string", "line": 0, "col": 0},
      "context": {
        "expected_type": "string",
        "found_type": "string"
      }
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "lex_errors": 0,
    "parse_errors": 0,
    "type_errors": 0,
    "warnings": 0
  }
}
```

**Symbol Table Schema:**
```json
{
  "symbols": [
    {
      "name": "string",
      "kind": "fn|let|type|param",
      "type": "string",
      "is_pure": true,
      "effects": ["IO", "FS"],
      "contracts": {
        "requires": ["expr"],
        "ensures": ["expr"]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

**Module Contract Schema:**
```json
{
  "module": "string",
  "capabilities": ["IO", "Net"],
  "public_functions": [
    {
      "name": "string",
      "signature": "string",
      "is_pure": true,
      "effects": ["IO"]
    }
  ],
  "imports": ["module1", "module2"],
  "types": [
    {
      "name": "string",
      "kind": "enum|alias",
      "variants": ["variant1", "variant2"]
    }
  ]
}
```

---

*Gradient Language Specification v0.1*
*For agent consumption and constrained decoding*
